title: mutelia officinalis
intro: Mutelia officinalis, also known as Prickly Ash or Toothache Tree, is a plant species native to North America, belonging to the family Rutaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and antiseptic compounds, which contribute to its health benefits, such as pain relief, reduction of fever, and wound healing. The bark and roots of the Mutelia officinalis plant are commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, to treat various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties have been utilized by indigenous communities and are now recognized by modern herbalism for its potential in alleviating toothaches, arthritis, and other inflammatory conditions.
